description: Graph RAG relationship design for the opinion analysis knowledge base. This file defines the schema-level relationship contract for Neo4j ingestion and query implementation.

confidence.description: Confidence of the extracted relationship. Low-confidence relations should not be used as core report evidence.

evidence_refs.description: Evidence IDs or source chunk IDs supporting this relationship.

source_path.description: Original markdown path from which the relationship was extracted.

extracted_by.description: Extractor name, script name, or human annotator.

relations.name: INVOLVES
relations.description: Connects a public opinion case to involved organizations, media, KOLs, people, or groups.

properties.role_in_case: responsible_party | regulator | media_amplifier | affected_group | observer | other
properties.stance: supportive | opposing | neutral | questioning | responding | unknown

relations.name: HAS_RISK_PATTERN
relations.description: Connects a case to detected or manually annotated risk patterns.

relations.name: USED_TACTIC
relations.description: Connects a case to response tactics used or recommended in the case.

relations.name: SUPPORTED_BY
relations.description: Links a case to evidence chunks used for traceability.

relations.name: MATCHES_SCENARIO
relations.description: Connects a case to the scenario it belongs to.

relations.name: SIMILAR_TO
relations.description: Connects similar cases for Graph RAG case matching and experience reuse.

relations.name: MITIGATED_BY
relations.description: Connects a risk pattern to tactics that can mitigate it.

relations.name: EXPLAINED_BY
relations.description: Connects risk patterns to theories or methodologies that explain them.

relations.name: GUIDES
relations.description: Connects methodologies to response tactics they can guide.

relations.name: RECOMMENDS
relations.description: Connects a domain playbook to recommended response tactics.

relations.name: REFERENCES
relations.description: Connects a domain playbook to example cases or references.

implementation_notes: Task 05 only defines the relationship contract. Neo4j write/query implementation belongs to Task 06.
All relationships should keep confidence and evidence_refs when available.
Relations with status=raw or low confidence should be treated as reference material, not as core report conclusions.
SIMILAR_TO can be computed later by combining domain, subdomain, event_type, key_claims, risk_patterns, and response_tactics.
